question: A modeler wants to attach a non-interrupting error boundary event to a service task in Camunda 8.8 so the task can continue while a handler runs in parallel. According to the Events section of the BPMN coverage page, is this supported?

options.text: Yes — non-interrupting error boundary events are the only error variant supported; interrupting error boundary events are not implemented.
No — in the Error row of the events coverage table, only the interrupting variants (event subprocess, boundary, and end) are implemented; the non-interrupting boundary column is empty.
Yes — every error event variant (start, catch, boundary interrupting and non-interrupting, throw) is implemented for the Error row.
No — error boundary events are not supported in Camunda 8.8 at all; teams must use a regular boundary timer to detect errors.

a.text: The relationship is reversed: the interrupting error boundary event is implemented; the non-interrupting one is not.

b.text: The Error row of the events coverage table shows implemented cells only for the Event Subprocess (interrupting) column, the Boundary (interrupting) column, and the End column. The Start, Event Subprocess non-interrupting, Catch, Boundary non-interrupting, and Throw cells for Error are empty.

c.text: The Error row is sparse: most cells are empty. Start, Catch, non-interrupting variants, and Throw are all unimplemented for errors.

d.text: Interrupting error boundary events are supported. A timer boundary is not the appropriate substitute for catching a business error.

explanation: In the BPMN coverage events table, the Error row only marks three cells as implemented: the interrupting event subprocess start event, the interrupting boundary event, and the end event. Non-interrupting error boundary events are not supported in Camunda 8.8; error handling on a still-running activity is not available. To react to an error you must either let the boundary event interrupt the activity or model the recoverable flow differently.
